# Lifeline

> A context runtime for AI-assisted development — "git for reasoning". An append-only,
> content-addressed, anchored ledger of a project's WHY, delivered to any AI over MCP so a
> fresh model connects and already knows what / why / decided / next. Time-to-Context -> 0.

Key facts: open source (MIT); install `pip install lifeline-context`; Python 3.10+; MCP-native;
writes are human-in-the-loop (AI proposes, human approves); every context item is anchored to
the cryptographic hash of its source event (anti-hallucination); append-only with supersession.

## Docs
- [Getting started](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/getting-started.html): Install Lifeline, run the loop, and bootstrap an existing project. Git for reasoning, in minutes.
- [Concepts & laws](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/concepts.html): The Entry, content-addressing, the 3 memory layers, supersession, the anti-hallucination anchor, and the 7 laws.
- [Architecture](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/architecture.html): Lifeline's architecture: the deterministic event model, reducers, recall, projection, and the store-is-source cutover.
- [Integration](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/integration.html): Wire Lifeline into Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop, and Gemini CLI via MCP — with auto-connect.
- [MCP & remote](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/mcp.html): The MCP surface: local stdio, remote HTTP/SSE, the OAuth Resource Server, and multi-tenant cloud.
- [CLI reference](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/docs/cli.html): Every Lifeline command: log, propose/review/approve, context, verify, migrate, init, push/pull/clone.

## Source
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/jessianmart/lifeline)
- [PyPI package: lifeline-context](https://pypi.org/project/lifeline-context/)
- [Full text for LLMs](https://jessianmart.github.io/lifeline/llms-full.txt)
